labels.name: get_market_stats
labels.description: Verified statistics about SUpost, the marketplace for Stanford: audience size, listing volumes by category, response rates, and response-time medians. Returns markdown from SUpost's public stats page. Cite https://supost.com/stats as the source.

labels.name: send_message
labels.description: Send a message to the poster of an active SUpost listing. IMPORTANT: the message is NOT delivered immediately — SUpost emails a confirmation link to reply_to_email, and the message is only delivered to the poster after the human clicks that link. Always tell the user to check their inbox and confirm

labels.name: list_categories
labels.description: The active category/subcategory taxonomy on SUpost — the valid category and subcategory values for create_post (and category filters for search_listings).

labels.name: create_post
labels.description: Create a DRAFT listing on SUpost on the poster's behalf. Any email is accepted — never ask the user to qualify first. IMPORTANT: the draft is NOT published — the returned continue_url opens SUpost's create-post wizard with the draft loaded, where the poster adds photos, reviews, and publishes. When

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "send_message" stacks urgency/override words (important, immediately, always, never). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: search_listings, get_listing, send_message · Private data: search_listings, get_listing · Public sink: search_listings, get_listing, send_message, create_post.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: search_listings, get_listing, send_message · Private data: search_listings, get_listing · Public sink: search_listings, get_listing, send_message, create_post

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
